title: Scope anthusbot auth to release/requirements push steps

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Investigating current workflows. requirements-sync commits as github-actions[bot], and release.yml semantic-release uses GITHUB_TOKEN, both blocked by protected main. Implementing scoped anthusbot token usage only for write steps.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Patched workflows to scope bot auth to write paths only: requirements-sync now uses anthusbot identity/token only in commit+push step; release.yml now runs semantic-release with anthusbot token and bot git identity, while checkout/build steps remain normal GitHub Actions context. YAML validated locally.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Investigated CI failure after release automation change (run 22876496241). Root cause is version skew introduced by semantic-release commit 299fa2c: rust/Cargo.toml and python/pyproject.toml were bumped to 0.3.0, but root VERSION remained 0.2.2. Python smoke path reads __version__ from VERSION file, so scenario 'Python and Rust report the same version' fails with Python 0.2.2 vs Rust 0.3.0.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implementing best-practice fix: update semantic-release configuration to use supported version file tracking key so root VERSION is updated on every release, and align current VERSION value with released 0.3.0 to restore Python/Rust version parity smoke test.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented fix in branch: switched [tool.semantic_release] from version_files to version_variables with plain-file format (VERSION:*:nf), and pinned CI install to python-semantic-release~=10.0 to reduce breaking config drift. Validated pyproject parses and semantic-release CLI runs without config errors.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Prepared operator steps for one-time VERSION repair via PR-only flow (no direct main push). Verified current remote skew persists on origin/main: VERSION=0.2.2 while python/rust manifest versions are 0.3.0.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Root cause verified via reproduction with python-semantic-release 10.5.3 debug logs: version_variables entry  compiles to a variable-assignment regex and records num_matches=0 on file VERSION, so the plain file value is never updated. Release 0.3.1 updated python/pyproject.toml + rust/Cargo.toml only; VERSION stayed 0.3.0, causing CI mismatch Python(0.3.0 from VERSION) vs Rust(0.3.1 from Cargo).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Root cause verified via reproduction with python-semantic-release 10.5.3 debug logs: version_variables entry "VERSION:*:nf" compiles to a variable-assignment regex and records num_matches=0 on file VERSION, so the plain file value is never updated. Release 0.3.1 updated python/pyproject.toml + rust/Cargo.toml only; VERSION stayed 0.3.0, causing CI mismatch Python(0.3.0 from VERSION) vs Rust(0.3.1 from Cargo).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implementing durable fix: convert VERSION file to explicit assignment syntax compatible with python-semantic-release version_variables, set precise variable key in config, and update Python/Rust VERSION readers in smoke paths to parse semver token robustly. This removes recurring mismatch risk in future releases.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented and validated durable version-sync fix. Changes: pyproject version_variables set to "VERSION:VERSION:nf"; VERSION file converted to assignment format; Python _read_version now extracts semver token robustly; Python smoke step and Rust cucumber version-file assertion now parse semver token from VERSION content. Validation: cargo test --test cucumber --no-run passed; Python smoke feature passed locally; semantic-release 10.5.3 dry-run on fresh main clone showed num_matches=1 for VERSION pattern and rewrote VERSION from VERSION = "0.3.1" to VERSION = "0.3.2".

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Post-merge stash apply validation: aligned VERSION to 0.3.1 assignment format (VERSION = "0.3.1"); verified python/rust manifest versions both 0.3.1; rust/Cargo.lock reflects virtuus 0.3.1. Validation green: cargo test --test cucumber --no-run and Python smoke feature behave run both pass.
